rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE EDWARD L. HEE AND BERNADETTE W. HEE LIVING TRUST DATED MARCH 12, 2002
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANK DIHARTCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/28/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to attorney with the following issues: 1) Petitioner alleges that notice to the California Victim Compensation Board under 9202(b) was provided on 2/4/25. Please allege who was incarcerated and whether notice was provided under Probate Code 216. 2) Petitioner provided conflicting information regarding the assets on hand. a. Par. 21 states that on 12/31/24, the assets on hand were $2,828,361.70. b. In par. 31, it states that the assets on hand as of 12/31/25 will be $2,877,110.00 (NB: the assets on hand cannot be for a future date). 3) Petitioner alleges that Sandrine Ourtiague and Jean-Pierre Maitia assigned 20% of their interest to American Research Bureau ("ARB"); however, although the assignment was filed by ARB, the actual assignment that was attached are assignments to Fraser & Fraser. 4) The proposed distribution must specify what each heir will receive after deducting amounts for assignments and what each assignee will receive from each heir, as well as the total amount they will receive from all their assignments. 5) Please confirm that the amounts distributable to each beneficiary will be made directly to that beneficiary. If direct distribution will not be made to the beneficiary, a copy of either the agreement or power of attorney must be filed allowing such distribution. 6) The 13100 Affidavit for Christian Ourtiague has not been filed. 7) Please provide a detailed schedule of receipts, gains and losses [California Rules of Court 7.705(b)] used in the fee base. 8) A revised proposed order should be filed after addressing the issues above.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARINA NIKOL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/30/25. No response to most of the issues posted for the hearing on 4/14/25. A "declaration" re personal service of notice was filed on 5/22/25, claiming that notice was given to the Court-appointed attorney, but no copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served, showing all the pertinent information about the hearing with the remote instructions attached to it, was attached to the "declaration." (The only correct form of proof of service is on the reverse side of a notice of hearing. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing must be attached to it.) If issues are not fully addressed in time to provide courtesy copies to the assigned examiner by 7/16/25, an appearance will be required on 7/30/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHERINE S. KIM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/30/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] 2. Paragraph 19 of the petition appears to be incomplete if there is now just cash on hand. What happened to the securities? [CRC 7.550(b).] 3. No receipt has been filed for the distribution of the vehicle. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.c.] 4. Paragraph 21 of the petition is contradicted by paragraph 22 of the petition. 5. The claim filed by Glen Davis was for $9,743.80, not $9,746.80. 6. If cash on hand totals $95,713.81, the net amount distributable to each heir does not appear to be correct. 7. The petition caption says the administrator is seeking compensation, but the second paragraph 30 of the petition indicates that he waived compensation. 8. The administrator was appointed on 3/20/23, not 2/23/23. 9. The creditor's claim period expired on 8/13/23 -- four months after Letters were issued on 4/13/23 -- not 6/23/23. (As Counsel was previously advised, Notice of Remote Appearance is not required in Probate cases, and is not the required notice to an interested party about how to appear remotely.) Please provide a courtesy copy of Administrator's verified amendment to his petition and the proof of service of the complete notice of the 7/30/25 hearing of the petition and the amendment to the petition to the assigned examiner by 7/16/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Also as previously noted: lack of courtesy copies and a proposed order, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E., can delay review. All pleadings must show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., Raymond H. Kim, Administrator. "Petitioner" is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN MCKEON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and determine: 1) The proposed administrator, a nominee of beneficiaries who are ineligibe for their own appointment due to their residence outside of the United States, is an attorney concurrently representing petitioner Niall MacLennan. It is unclear whether Niall MacLennan is a beneficiary of the estate of his mother Margaret MacLennan (postdeceased beneficiary of this estate). If not, Niall MacLennan has no interest in this estate and appears to lack standing to file this petition. If he is a beneficiary of his mother'e estate, the petition failed to address whether the nominee-attorney as proposed administrator anticipates a conflict of interest under Probate Code section 8465(d)(1). Is the attorney capable of serving as administrator with a fiduciary duty of impartiality owed to all the beneficiaries, while continuing his representation of Niall MacLennan with a duty to advocate his individual interest? 2) The petition failed to address section 8465(e) requiring the Court to make specific findings of fact in support of good cause to waive the administrator's bond, nor did the petition address other alternatives (e.g., blocked account) the Court is required to consider before waiving the bond. The mere bond waivers signed by all the beneficiaries do not constitute good cause under section 8465(e). It should noted that the foregoing issues were previously raised by the Court before the hearing on the prior petiton in October 2024 and were not addressed for more than 6 months before the prior petition was eventually taken off calendar at the hearing on 04/28/2025. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HARRY JOSEPH BOBINEAUX
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/28/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of publication of the Notice of AMENDED Petition to Administer Estate has been filed. (3) Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be answered. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the amended petition and any other papers to the assigned examiner by 7/14/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Petitioner should dismiss her petition filed in case PES-24-308336, as it appears to be for this same decedent and the numerous issues with that petition still have not been addressed.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE 1993 BORSUK FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: (1) Compelling Trustee To Distribute Trust Assets To Beneficiaries; (2) Barring Trustee'S Use Of Trust Funds For Litigation; And (3) Awarding Petitioner Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/30/25. Still no proposed order has been lodged or delivered to the assigned examiner. Paragraph 2 of the prayer requests distribution of assets as requested in paragraph 28 of the petition, but paragraph 28 of the petition references attorney fees and costs. It does not appear that the surviving settlor had authority to amend paragraph 6.4 of the trust; the Bypass Trust became irrevocable upon the death of the first settlor. No courtesy copy of the objections filed on 6/3/25 has been received by the examiner either. The matter will not be heard until a proposed order is lodged or submitted. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE IRREVOCABLE TRUST FBO SUZANNE BORSUK
rulings.Calendar Matter: (1) Suspending And Removing Trustee; 920 Apponting Successor Trustee; (3) Compelling An Accounting; And (4) Barring Trustee'S Use Of Trust Founds For Litigation
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/30/25. Still no proposed order has been lodged or delivered to the assigned examiner. The petition does not disclose whom the Court should appoint as the successor trustee. Such person should consent. It does not appear that the surviving settlor had authority to amend paragraph 6.4 of the trust; the Bypass Trust became irrevocable upon the death of the first settlor. No courtesy copy of the "supplemental objection" filed on 6/3/25 has been received by the examiner either. The matter will not be heard until a proposed order is lodged or submitted. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROGER PHILLIPPE BROSSARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing,, although item 8 of the petition did not set forth the RELATIONSHIP to the decedent of the persons listed. Status of closing the estate will be set for 6/29/26. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a further petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 6/29/26. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed.
